OrADE Constitution

Article I Name

The name of this organization shall be: Oregon Association of Disability Employees (OrADE). OrADE is an affiliate of the National Association of Disability Examiners (NADE).

Article II Purpose

Section 1. The primary purpose of OrADE shall be to advance disability adjudication as a multi-disciplinary science and as a profession.

Section 2. The further purpose of OrADE shall be to promote community and professional understanding of disability programs and processes.

Article III Qualification of Members

Membership in the chapter shall be open to any person, corporation or organization in good standing as a member of NADE.

Section 1. Classes of Membership

There shall be three classes of membership: Full, Retired, and Honorary.

Full members are current dues paying members of the association.

Retired members are retired employees of any agency engaged in providing services related to adjudication of disability claims including any support services person of that agency whose dues to the association are currently paid.

Honorary members are persons given membership by the executive board. These members do not pay dues and have no voting privileges.

Section 2. Privileges of Membership

A. All members of the chapter shall receive copies of meeting minutes, news letters and all other publications of the chapter.

B. Full members shall be eligible to serve as members of the delegate assembly, as officers of the chapter, and as a member of the Executive Board.

Article IV Officers/Executive Board

A: Officers of the chapter are: President, President-elect, Secretary, Treasurer and Historian. They shall serve terms as specified in the By-Laws.

B: The executive board shall consist of the officers plus the past president who will have no voting rights.

Article V Meetings

The executive board and membership shall meet regularly as defined in the By-Laws or as called by the President or executive board.

Article VI Amendments

The constitution and By-Laws of the chapter may be amended as follows:

A: An amendment may be proposed by a majority of the executive board; or

B: An amendment may be presented to the executive board by any member.

C: Amendments shall be voted on by the membership. Ballots shall be provided to all voting members and counted by a team of two people appointed by the President. If adopted by a majority of those casting votes, amendments shall become effective immediately.

Revised 4/8/2004

## By-laws of the Oregon Association of Disability Employees

Article I. Accomplishment of Purpose

The purpose of this organization, as designated in its constitution, shall be accomplished through:

A: Training for members and

B: Programs designed to acquaint others with disability claims procedures.

Article II Quorum

A: A quorum shall be one fourth of the current voting members.

B: A quorum of the executive board is three of the five voting members.

Article III Officers

A: The term of all offices shall be one year.

B: The President shall:

1. Chair the executive board.

1. Preside over all official meetings.
2. Appoint members to committees.
3. Enforce the timelines in the calendar of events maintained by the president-elect.
4. Serve as the official representative of the chapter.
5. For one year after term, serve as the past president member of executive board
6. Be subject to impeachment by unanimous vote of the other members of the executive board for failing to carry out the duties of president.

C: The President-elect shall:

1. Assume the office of president at the end of the term as president-elect or earlier if the president is unwilling or unable to complete their term of office.
2. Be an ex-officio member of all chapter committees and serve as a resource to all committees.
3. Actively participate in the National Disability Professionals Week planning and execution.
4. Maintain a calendar of chapter events and timeline of due dates for regional or national events and required reports.
5. Preside over all regularly scheduled chapter meetings the president does not attend.
6. Be a member of the executive board.

D: The Secretary shall:

1. Serve in that office for no more than two consecutive one year terms.

1. Record and publish minutes of all official meetings of the chapter.
2. Maintain official records of the chapter meetings and annually forward these records to the Historian for archives.

E: The Treasurer shall:

1. Serve in that office for no more than two consecutive one year terms.

2. Safeguard the funds of the chapter.

3. Disburse funds as ordered by the president or executive board.

1. Present a current financial report at chapter and executive board meetings.
2. Maintain the chapter financial records and forward these records to the Historian for archives after the annual audit.

F:         The Historian shall:

                        1.    Serve in that office for no more than two consecutive one year terms.

            2.    Act as custodian and keep an inventory of all  equipment and supplies.

            3.    Keep copy of all  archived Board and OrADE meeting minutes.

            4.    Keep copy of all  archived Treasurer’s notes and reports.

            5.    Keep copy  of all NADE ADVOCATEs, Pacific Wave Newsletters, and maintain all manuals, photograph albums,  and anything published in the name of OrADE.

Article IV Executive Board

A: The executive board shall:

1. Meet no less than four times each year, the time and place of meetings to be defined by the president.
2. Plan, publicize and sponsor meetings of membership.
3. Administer the business affairs of the organization.
4. Supervise or delegate advertising, promotion, fund raising or training programs for the membership or the community.
5. Arrange for an annual audit of the chapter financial records to be done either by an outside auditor or two members of the chapter who do not have signature authority.

B: The executive board shall consist of the officers plus the past president who will have no voting rights.

Article V Meetings

A: Chapter meetings will be held as designated by the President or executive board.

B: An annual meeting for the purpose of election of officers and for the executive board members to report on their activities over the year, shall be held at a time and place determined by the President.

C: Special meetings can be called by the president or three voting members of the executive board.

Article VI Elections

A: Any full member can declare their intent to run for office by notifying any executive board member at any time prior to the mailing of ballots. A list of all candidates will be sent to chapter members at least 30 days in advance of the annual meeting in which the elections will occur. A ballot will be sent with the list.

B: Ballots shall be counted by a team of two appointed by the president and reported at the April chapter meeting. Election will be determined by simple majority of the votes cast by a quorum of members.

C: The executive board can fill the positions of people who resign or are unable to fulfill duties of office by vote of three members of the executive board. Officers elected in this manner will take office at the next regular chapter meeting and serve the remainder of that term of office.

Article VII Parliamentary Authority

The rules contained in Roberts Rules of Order shall govern in all cases in which they are applicable, and in which they are consistent with the By-Laws.

Revised 4/8/2004
